generatedSentence: Kael, an outcast machinist in a voxel-rendered workshop, crafted novelties from salvaged ornament and delicate belongings. His hackney existence was lacking ambition, an apathy he’d long objected to in others, yet it clung to him. Despite his virtue, his job felt phony, a mere formality. He remembered his moms’ old blackboards filled with the alphabet, lessons a bright student would grasp, not him. After a lonely dinner of cleaning his tools by hand, he’d often gaze at a broken nautilus shell, dreaming of aeronautic escapes on his salvaged bike. A jarring notification on his ancient data-log provided the first indication of the looming danger. The global kingpin, known only as L, and his brutal killjoys were initiating a process of rollbacking human sentience, promising an era of karmic apathy under the guise of safety. This wasn't just a threat to his own safety; it was an enormous obstacle to the collective virtue. The urgency to transport a vital, unfinished device, a prototype for sensory recalibration, became Kael's primary concern. The kingpin’s omnipresent merchandising, a deluge of munchy sustenance and phony smiles, was a cuckoo in every nest, slowly eroding the slack season of peace. Kael, now donning a unique viola-colored jumpsuit, understood the stakes. The kingpin’s power, nearly omniscient, employed killers who left a trail of grime, their intentions as clear as glass. His traditional nonviolence was challenged; this wasn’t just a decline, it was a total collapse. He had to uphold virtue against this tremendous darkness. As he moved, his bike's automatic hum was the only sound, cutting through the silence. Suddenly, a monstrous megalodon phantasm, a product of the kingpin’s mind-control, rose from the groundwater, its spectral tongues speaking a repeated warning, its enormous eyesockets fixed on him. Kael's mind was aback, but his hand moved with purpose, remembering the Greeks’ stories of legendary battles, ready to ignite the device and avert the impending requiem. With a meritorious flourish, Kael activated the device, its luculent glow illuminating the western outback, turning the grime to glitter. The phantasm, caught in the feedback, began to flakey, its black knots unraveling like unspooled thread. The kingpin’s control weakened, his omnipresent notification systems going silent. Kael felt the automatic backslapping of success, a true triumph over the grumpy, phony monarch. The process, though difficult, had worked. His belongings were scattered, but his purpose was clear: to maintain freedom. He envisioned blueberries blooming in the season, a gardener tending them, a feast for all, a happy contrast to the kingpin’s eggplant-colored regime. He had, through action, kept the future safe, narrating a new dialogue of hope for all.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A skinny figure in a viola-colored jumpsuit with intricate circuit patterns, wearing a silver-chrome helmet with a single glowing blue visor. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Kael, the protagonist, sits on the ground in a dimly lit, cluttered workshop, tinkering with a glowing blue device, focused, surrounded by abstract, glowing voxel tools. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Kael, riding a sleek, futuristic bike, speeds through a neon-lit, dystopian city street at night, looking over his shoulder at a distant, towering holographic billboard displaying a grinning, shadowy figure (the kingpin). BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A colossal, translucent megalodon-like phantasm, composed of swirling energy and glowing eyes, erupts from a cracked, glowing urban street, looming over Kael, who is on his bike, a look of intense determination on his face as he clutches the blue device. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Kael stands victorious amidst dissipating energy effects and fading, spectral fragments of the phantasm, holding the brightly glowing device aloft, a hopeful sunrise breaking over a clean, revitalized futuristic landscape.

definitions.definition: To disagree with or oppose something or someone; (especially in a Court of Law) to raise an objection.
definitions.example: I object to the proposal to build a new airport terminal.

definitions.definition: To offer in opposition as a criminal charge or by way of accusation or reproach; to adduce as an objection or adverse reason.
To set before or against; to bring into opposition; to oppose.
One who has total knowledge.
Having total knowledge.
definitions.example: The Abrahamic God is omniscient.

definitions.definition: Something seen but having no physical reality; a phantom or apparition.
The condition or feeling of being safe; security; certainty.
definitions.example: If you push it to the limit, safety is not guaranteed.

definitions.definition: A mechanism on a weapon or dangerous equipment designed to prevent accidental firing.
definitions.example: Be sure that the safety is set before proceeding.

definitions.definition: An instance of a player being sacked or tackled in the end zone, or stepping out of the end zone and off the field, resulting in two points to the opposite team.
definitions.example: He sacked the quarterback in the end zone for a safety.

definitions.definition: Any of the defensive players who are in position furthest from the line of scrimmage and whose responsibility is to defend against passes as well as to be the tacklers of last resort.
definitions.example: The free safety made a game-saving tackle on the runner who had broken past the linebackers.

definitions.definition: A safety squeeze.
Preservation from escape; close custody.
A safety bicycle.
To secure (a mechanical component, as in aviation) to keep it from becoming detached even under vibration.
To secure a firing pin, as in guns, to keep the gun from firing
The status or position of a performer acknowledged to be a star; fame; celebrity.
(motorized vehicles) The axis around which steered wheels pivot; a bolt that holds the axis in place—a kingbolt.
The pin at the centre of the triangle of bowling pins (originally the tallest pin in kayles). Sometimes also the headpin at the apex.
The most important person in an undertaking or organization.
The bolt holding together the truck of a roller skate or skateboard.
A person who is anti-fun, or prevents others from having fun.
A constructor of machines and engines; one versed in the principles of machines.
One skilled in the use of machine tools for fashioning metal parts or tools out of metal.
A person who operates machinery.
A person employed to shift scenery in a theater.
Accordance with moral principles; conformity of behaviour or thought with the strictures of morality; good moral conduct.
definitions.example: Without virtue, there is no freedom.

definitions.definition: A particular manifestation of moral excellence in a person; an admirable quality.
Specifically, each of several qualities held to be particularly important, including the four cardinal virtues, the three theological virtues, or the seven virtues opposed to the seven deadly sins.
An inherently advantageous or excellent quality of something or someone; a favourable point, an advantage.
A creature embodying divine power, specifically one of the orders of heavenly beings, traditionally ranked above angels and below archangels.
Specifically, moral conduct in sexual behaviour, especially of women; chastity.
The inherent power of a god, or other supernatural being.
The inherent power or efficacy of something (now only in phrases).
Towards the back or rear; backwards.
In the rear; a distance behind.
By surprise; startled; dumbfounded. (see usage)
Backward against the mast; said of the sails when pressed by the wind from the "wrong" (forward) side, or of a ship when its sails are set that way.
The state of being new or novel; newness.
A new product; an innovation.
A small mass-produced trinket.
In novelty theory, newness, density of complexification, and dynamic change as opposed to static habituation.

A marine mollusc, of the family Nautilidae native to the Pacific Ocean and Indian Ocean, which has tentacles and a spiral shell with a series of air-filled chambers, of which Nautilus is the type genus.
A kind of diving bell that sinks or rises by means of compressed air.
The quality or condition of being urgent
definitions.example: the urgency of a demand

definitions.definition: Insistence, pressure
definitions.example: I have this sudden urgency to use the toilet.

definitions.definition: An act of transporting; conveyance.
definitions.example: The transport of goods is not included in the price given on the website.

definitions.definition: The state of being transported by emotion; rapture.
A vehicle used to transport (passengers, mail, freight, troops etc.)
A tractor-trailer.
The system of transporting passengers, etc. in a particular region; the vehicles used in such a system.
definitions.example: The local transport received a big boost as part of the mayor's infrastructural plans.

definitions.definition: A device that moves recording tape across the read/write heads of a tape recorder or video recorder etc.
A deported convict.
To carry or bear from one place to another; to remove; to convey.
definitions.example: to transport goods; to transport troops

definitions.definition: To deport to a penal colony.
To move (someone) to strong emotion; to carry away.
definitions.example: Music transports the soul.

definitions.definition: A globally scoped identifier.
Concerning all parts of the world.
definitions.example: Pollution is a global problem.

definitions.definition: Pertaining to the whole of something; total, universal:
Spherical, ball-shaped.
definitions.example: In the center was a small, global mass.

definitions.definition: Of or relating to a globe or sphere.
In the global manner; world-wide.
An inhabitant, resident, or person of descent from Greece.

Unintelligible speech or text, such as foreign speech or text, or regarding subjects the listener is not familiar with, such as mathematics or technical jargon; or statements that the listener does not understand or agree with.
A member of a college fraternity or sorority, which are characterised by being named after Greek letters. (See also Greek system.)
definitions.example: Was Joe a Greek in college?

definitions.definition: A cunning rogue; a merry fellow.
Anal sex.
Greek cuisine; traditional Greek food.
(chiefly in the plural) One of the Greeks, measures of derivative price sensitivity.
To display a placeholder (instead of text), especially to optimize speed in displaying text that would be too small to read.
To fill a template with nonsense text (particularly the Lorem ipsum), so that form can be focused on instead of content.
Of or pertaining to karma.
To engage in trade; to carry on commerce.
To engage in in-store promotion of the sale of goods, as by display and arrangement of goods.
definitions.example: He started his career merchandising in a small clothing store chain.

definitions.definition: To engage in the trade of.
To engage in in-store promotion of the sale of.
definitions.example: He got hired to merchandise some new sporting goods lines.

definitions.definition: To promote as if for sale.
definitions.example: The record companies don't get as good a return on merchandising artists under contract.

definitions.definition: Trade in merchandise

The three-dimensional analogue of a pixel; a volume element representing some numerical quantity, such as the colour, of a point in three-dimensional space, used in the visualisation and analysis of three-dimensional (especially scientific and medical) data
To set fire to (something), to light (something)
To spark off (something), to trigger
To commence burning.
To subject to the action of intense heat; to heat strongly; often said of incombustible or infusible substances.

definitions.definition: An ordinary horse.
A carriage for hire or a cab.
A horse used to ride or drive.
A breed of English horse.
A hired drudge; a hireling; a prostitute.
To make uninteresting or trite by frequent use.
To use as a hackney.
To carry in a hackney coach.
Offered for hire; hence, much used; trite; mean.

definitions.definition: A delicate item of clothing, especially underwear or lingerie.

definitions.definition: A choice dainty; a delicacy.
A delicate, luxurious, or effeminate person.
Easily damaged or requiring careful handling.

definitions.definition: Characterized by a fine structure or thin lines.
definitions.example: Her face was delicate.

definitions.definition: Intended for use with fragile items.
definitions.example: Set the washing machine to the delicate cycle.

definitions.definition: Refined; gentle; scrupulous not to trespass or offend; considerate; said of manners, conduct, or feelings.

definitions.definition: Of weak health; easily sick; unable to endure hardship.

definitions.definition: Unwell, especially because of having drunk too much alcohol.
definitions.example: Please don't speak so loudly: I'm feeling a bit delicate this morning.

definitions.definition: Addicted to pleasure; luxurious; voluptuous; alluring.
Pleasing to the senses; refined; adapted to please an elegant or cultivated taste.

definitions.definition: Slight and shapely; lovely; graceful.
Light, or softly tinted; said of a colour.

definitions.definition: Of exacting tastes and habits; dainty; fastidious.
Highly discriminating or perceptive; refinedly critical; sensitive; exquisite.

definitions.definition: Affected by slight causes; showing slight changes.
definitions.example: a delicate thermometer

definitions.definition: The feeling that one belongs.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the plural) Something physical that is owned.

definitions.definition: Family; relations; household.
An element of decoration; that which embellishes or adorns.
A Christmas tree decoration.
A musical flourish that is unnecessary to the overall melodic or harmonic line, but serves to decorate or "ornament" that line.
(in the plural) The articles used in church services.
A characteristic that has a decorative function (typically in order to attract a mate)
To decorate.

definitions.definition: To add to.
definitions.example: The editor ornamented his plain writing, making it fancier but less clear.

definitions.definition: A ship used to transport people, smaller vehicles and goods from one port to another, usually on a regular schedule.
A place where passengers are transported across water in such a ship.
The legal right or franchise that entitles a corporate body or an individual to operate such a service.
To carry; transport; convey.
definitions.example: Trucks plowed through the water to ferry flood victims to safety.

definitions.definition: To move someone or something from one place to another, usually repeatedly.
definitions.example: Being a good waiter takes more than the ability to ferry plates of food around a restaurant.

definitions.definition: To carry or transport over a contracted body of water, as a river or strait, in a boat or other floating conveyance plying between opposite shores.
To pass over water in a boat or by ferry.
A mass (especially Catholic) to honor and remember a dead person.
A musical composition for such a mass.
A piece of music composed to honor a dead person.
Rest; peace
A symbol in an alphabet.

definitions.definition: A written or printed communication, generally longer and more formal than a note.
definitions.example: I wrote a letter to my sister about my life.

definitions.definition: The literal meaning of something, as distinguished from its intended and remoter meaning (often contrasted with the spirit).
(plural) Literature.
definitions.example: Benjamin Franklin was multiskilled – a scientist, politician and a man of letters.

definitions.definition: A division unit of a piece of law marked by a letter of the alphabet.
A size of paper, 8½ in × 11 in (215.9 mm × 279.4 mm, US paper sizes rounded to the nearest 5 mm).
A size of paper, 215 mm × 280 mm.
A single type; type, collectively; a style of type.

One who lets, or lets out.

definitions.definition: One who retards or hinders.
Literature (school subject).

definitions.definition: The liberal arts, humanities, learning (broad accumulated cultural knowledge).
definitions.example: Though architecture in ancient Rome was not generally considered to belong to the artes liberales, Vitruvius easily qualified as a man of letters because of his wide learning.

definitions.definition: An identifying initial of the academic institution that is awarded to a member of a varsity team. It is made from structured cloth, and intended to be sewn to a piece of clothing such as a varsity sweater or varsity jacket.
To be without, to need, to require.
definitions.example: My life lacks excitement.

definitions.definition: To be short (of or for something).
definitions.example: He'll never lack for company while he's got all that money.

definitions.definition: To be in want.
To see the deficiency in (someone or something); to find fault with, to malign, reproach.
The absence of something; a lack.
Missing or not having enough of (a good quality, etc).
definitions.example: This cheese is lacking in pungency.

definitions.definition: Not carrying a firearm.
definitions.example: Are you packing or lacking?

definitions.definition: Litre, liter
Pound (sterling) (from Latin libra)
(familiar) mother.
An adult female owner of a pet.
Affectionate term of address for one's mother.
A stringed instrument of the violin family, somewhat larger than a violin, played under the chin, and having a deeper tone.
An organ stop having a similar tone.
A 10-string steel-string acoustic guitar, used in Brazilian folk music.
A berimbau viola, the smallest member of the berimbau used in capoeira music.
To preserve (food) in a jar.
To knock, shake, or strike sharply, especially causing a quivering or vibrating movement.
definitions.example: He hit it with a hammer, hoping he could jar it loose.

definitions.definition: To harm or injure by such action.
To shock or surprise.
definitions.example: I think the accident jarred him, as he hasn’t got back in a car since.

definitions.definition: To act in disagreement or opposition, to clash, to be at odds with; to interfere; to dispute, to quarrel.
To (cause something to) give forth a rudely tremulous or quivering sound; to (cause something to) sound discordantly or harshly.
definitions.example: The clashing notes jarred on my ears.

definitions.definition: To quiver or vibrate due to being shaken or struck.
Of the appearance, form, style, etc., of people and things: to look strangely different; to stand out awkwardly from its surroundings; to be incongruent.
Storage in jars.
definitions.example: The jarring of peaches took place at the end of the season.

definitions.definition: A feeling or movement that jars or jolts.
That jars (clashes or disagrees); incongruous, conflictful.
Act of pointing out or indicating.
That which serves to indicate or point out; mark; token; sign; symptom; evidence.
Discovery made; information.
Explanation; display.
Any symptom or occurrence in a disease, which serves to direct to suitable remedies.
An declared approximation of the price at which a traded security is likely to commence trading.
Lack of emotion or motivation; lack of interest or enthusiasm towards something; disinterest (in something).
Having to do with aerial navigation
A midday meal (in a context in which the evening meal is called supper or tea).
The main meal of the day, often eaten in the evening.
An evening meal.
A meal given to an animal.
definitions.example: Give the dog its dinner.

definitions.definition: A formal meal for many people eaten for a special occasion.
The food provided or consumed at any such meal.
To eat a dinner.
To provide (someone) with a dinner.
To remove dirt from a place or object.
definitions.example: Can you clean the windows today?

definitions.definition: To tidy up, make a place neat.
definitions.example: Clean your room right now!

definitions.definition: To remove equipment from a climbing route after it was previously lead climbed.
To make things clean in general.
definitions.example: She just likes to clean. That’s why I married her.

definitions.definition: To remove unnecessary files, etc. from (a directory, etc.).
To brush the ice lightly in front of a moving rock to remove any debris and ensure a correct line; less vigorous than a sweep.
To purge a raw of any blemishes caused by the scanning process such as brown tinting and poor color contrast.
To remove guts and/or scales of a butchered animal.
(gerund of clean) The process of making something clean.
The afterbirth of cows, ewes, etc.

That has been cast out; banished, ostracized.
To hold up; to lift on high; to elevate.

To support by approval or encouragement, to confirm (something which has been questioned)
Not finished, not completed.
To come back to life after having died.
To become undead.
Permanent; never-ending; infinite

definitions.definition: Any member of the kingdom Fungi; a eukaryotic organism typically having chitin cell walls but no chlorophyll or plastids. Fungi may be unicellular or multicellular.
A spongy, abnormal excrescence, such as excessive granulation tissue formed in a wound.
Of or relating to fable, myth or legend.
Characteristic of fables; marvelous, extraordinary, incredible.
Fictional or not believable; made up.
Known for telling fables or falsehoods; unreliable.
Very good; outstanding, wonderful.
Gay or pertaining to gay people.
Camp, effeminate.
Fashionable, glamorous

The flexible muscular organ in the mouth that is used to move food around, for tasting and that is moved into various positions to modify the flow of air from the lungs in order to produce different sounds in speech.
This organ, as taken from animals used for food (especially cows).−

definitions.definition: (metonym) A language.

Voice (the distinctive sound of a person's speech); accent (distinctive manner of pronouncing a language).
Manner of speaking, often habitually.

The power of articulate utterance; speech generally.
Discourse; fluency of speech or expression.
Discourse; fluency of speech or expression.
Honourable discourse; eulogy.
(often in the plural) Glossolalia.
In a shoe, the flap of material that goes between the laces and the foot (so called because it resembles a tongue in the mouth).

A projection, or slender appendage or fixture.
A long, narrow strip of land, projecting from the mainland into a sea or lake.
The pole of a vehicle; especially, the pole of an ox cart, to the end of which the oxen are yoked.

A reed.

On a wind instrument, to articulate a note by starting the air with a tap of the tongue, as though by speaking a 'd' or 't' sound (alveolar plosive).

To do or say again (and again).

To happen again; recur.
To echo the words of (a person).
To strike the hours, as a watch does.

To repay or refund (an excess received).

(normally said of a rope) Lax; not tense; not firmly extended.

definitions.definition: Weak; not holding fast.

definitions.definition: Lacking diligence or care; not earnest or eager.

definitions.definition: Not active,successful, or violent.

definitions.definition: Excess; surplus to requirements.

definitions.definition: Vulgar; sexually explicit, especially in dancehall music.
Slackly.

definitions.definition: Each of the four divisions of a year: spring, summer, autumn (fall) and winter
A part of a year when something particular happens
definitions.example: mating season

definitions.definition: That which gives relish; seasoning.
The period over which a series of Test matches are played.
A group of episodes of a television or radio program broadcast in regular intervals with a long break between each group, usually with one year between the beginning of each.
definitions.example: The third season of Friends aired from 1996 to 1997.

definitions.definition: An extended, undefined period of time.
The full set of downloadable content for a game, which can be purchased with a season pass.
A fixed period of time in a massively multiplayer online game in which new content (themes, rules, modes, etc.) becomes available, sometimes replacing earlier content.
To make fit for any use by time or habit; to habituate; to accustom; to inure.
definitions.example: to season oneself to a climate

definitions.definition: (by extension) To prepare by drying or hardening, or removal of natural juices.
definitions.example: The timber needs to be seasoned.

definitions.definition: To become mature; to grow fit for use; to become adapted to a climate.
To become dry and hard, by the escape of the natural juices, or by being penetrated with other substance.
definitions.example: The wood has seasoned in the sun.

definitions.definition: To mingle: to moderate, temper, or qualify by admixture.
To copulate with; to impregnate.
One who or that which kills.
definitions.example: Carbon monoxide is a silent killer.

definitions.definition: That which causes stress or is extremely difficult, especially that which may cause failure at a task.
definitions.example: That test was a killer.

definitions.definition: Something that is so far ahead of its competition that it effectively kills off that competition.
definitions.example: Digital streaming platforms are CD killers.

definitions.definition: A knockout form of darts or pool involving several players.
A club used for killing fish.
A particularly heavy type of handstamp, or portion of one, often obscuring a large part of the postage stamp.

A diacritical mark used in Indic scripts to suppress an inherent vowel (e.g., the Hindi viram, the Bengali or Oriya hasanta) or render the entire syllable silent (e.g., the Burmese virama, the Khmer toandakhiat).
A sea mammal related to dolphins and porpoises (Orcinus orca).
A looping of a piece of string or of any other long, flexible material that cannot be untangled without passing one or both ends of the material through its loops.
definitions.example: Climbers must make sure that all knots are both secure and of types that will not weaken the rope.

definitions.definition: (of hair, etc) A tangled clump.
definitions.example: The nurse was brushing knots from the protesting child's hair.

definitions.definition: A maze-like pattern.
A non-self-intersecting closed curve in (e.g., three-dimensional) space that is an abstraction of a knot (in sense 1 above).
definitions.example: A knot can be defined as a non-self-intersecting broken line whose endpoints coincide: when such a knot is constrained to lie in a plane, then it is simply a polygon.

definitions.definition: A difficult situation.
definitions.example: I got into a knot when I inadvertently insulted a policeman.

definitions.definition: The whorl left in lumber by the base of a branch growing out of the tree's trunk.
definitions.example: When preparing to tell stories at a campfire, I like to set aside a pile of pine logs with lots of knots, since they burn brighter and make dramatic pops and cracks.

definitions.definition: Local swelling in a tissue area, especially skin, often due to injury.
definitions.example: Jeremy had a knot on his head where he had bumped it on the bedframe.

definitions.definition: A protuberant joint in a plant.
Any knob, lump, swelling, or protuberance.
The swelling of the bulbus glandis in members of the dog family, Canidae
The point on which the action of a story depends; the gist of a matter.
definitions.example: the knot of the tale

definitions.definition: A node.
A kind of epaulet; a shoulder knot.
A group of people or things.
A bond of union; a connection; a tie.

A unit of speed, equal to one nautical mile per hour. (From the practice of counting the number of knots in the log-line (as it is paid out) in a standard time. Traditionally spaced at one every 1/120 of a mile.)
definitions.example: Cedric claimed his old yacht could make 12 knots.

definitions.definition: A nautical mile
The bulbus glandis
In omegaverse fiction, a bulbus glandis-like structure on the penis of a male alpha, which ties him to an omega during intercourse.
To form into a knot; to tie with a knot or knots.
definitions.example: We knotted the ends of the rope to keep it from unravelling.

definitions.definition: To form wrinkles in the forehead, as a sign of concentration, concern, surprise, etc.
definitions.example: She knotted her brow in concentration while attempting to unravel the tangled strands.

definitions.definition: To unite closely; to knit together.
To entangle or perplex; to puzzle.
To form knots.
To knit knots for a fringe.
One of a variety of shore birds; the red-breasted sandpiper (variously Calidris canutus or Tringa canutus).

A species of extinct shark that lived during the Miocene and Pliocene epochs. The species' scientific name is Carcharocles megalodon, although a few scientists argue the genus Otodus is more accurate.
A snack.
The part of the forelimb below the forearm or wrist in a human, and the corresponding part in many other animals.
definitions.example: Her hands are really strong.

definitions.definition: That which resembles, or to some extent performs the office of, a human hand.
In linear measurement:
A side; part, camp; direction, either right or left.
Power of performance; means of execution; ability; skill; dexterity.
An agent; a servant, or manual laborer, especially in compounds; a workman, trained or competent for special service or duty; a performer more or less skillful.
definitions.example: an old hand at speaking;  large farms need many farm hands

definitions.definition: An instance of helping.
definitions.example: Bob gave Alice a hand to move the furniture.

definitions.definition: Handwriting; style of penmanship.
definitions.example: a good hand

definitions.definition: A person's autograph or signature.
definitions.example: Given under my Hand and Seal of the State this 1st Day of January, 2010.

definitions.definition: Personal possession; ownership.
(usually in the plural, hands) Management, domain, control.
definitions.example: in safe hands;  in good hands;  He lost his job when the factory changed hands.  With the business back in the founder's hands, there is new hope for the company.  With John in charge of the project, it's in good hands.

definitions.definition: That which is, or may be, held in a hand at once.
Applause.
definitions.example: Give him a hand.

definitions.definition: A Native American gambling game, involving guessing the whereabouts of bits of ivory or similar, which are passed rapidly from hand to hand.
The small part of a gunstock near the lock, which is grasped by the hand in taking aim.
A whole rhizome of ginger.
The feel of a fabric; the impression or quality of the fabric as judged qualitatively by the sense of touch.
definitions.example: This fabric has a smooth, soft hand.

definitions.definition: Actual performance; deed; act; workmanship; agency; hence, manner of performance.
Agency in transmission from one person to another.
definitions.example: to buy at first hand (from the producer, or when new);  to buy at second hand (when no longer in the producer’s hand, or when not new);  It's not a rumor. I heard it at first hand.

definitions.definition: Rate; price.
To give, pass, or transmit with the hand, literally or figuratively.
definitions.example: He handed them the letter.   She handed responsibility over to her deputy.

definitions.definition: To lead, guide, or assist with the hand; to conduct.
definitions.example: to hand a lady into a carriage

definitions.definition: To manage.
To seize; to lay hands on.
To pledge by the hand; to handfast.
(said of a sail) To furl.
To cooperate.
A vehicle that has two wheels, one behind the other, a steering handle, and a saddle seat or seats and is usually propelled by the action of a rider’s feet upon pedals.
A traveling block used on a cable in skidding logs.
The best possible hand in lowball.
A motorbike.
(usually in compounds specifying a context) a slut; a promiscuous woman
To ride a bike.
definitions.example: I biked so much yesterday that I'm very sore today.

definitions.definition: To travel by bike.
definitions.example: It was such a nice day I decided to bike to the store, though it's far enough I usually take my car.

definitions.definition: To transport by bicycle
A motorcycle.
A small and light motorcycle.
A local woman who is sexually promiscuous.
An edible round berry, belonging to the cowberry group (Vaccinium sect. Cyanococcus), with flared crowns at the end, that turns blue on ripening.
The shrub of the above-mentioned berry.
A dark blue colour.
To gather or forage for blueberries.
An arboreal anthropoid ape genus Pongo consisting of two species, Pongo pygmaeus of Borneo and Pongo abelii of Sumatra, having a shaggy reddish-brown coat, long arms, and no tail.
A person who assumes an identity or quality other than their own.
definitions.example: He claims to be a doctor, but he's nothing but a fast-talking phony.

definitions.definition: A person who professes beliefs or opinions that they do not hold.
definitions.example: He's such a phony, he doesn't believe half of what he says.

definitions.definition: Anything fraudulent or fake.
Fraudulent; fake; having a misleading appearance.
definitions.example: A good jeweler should be able to tell a real stone from a phony one.

definitions.definition: To apply fibreglass to.
definitions.example: to fibreglass the hull of a fishing-boat

definitions.definition: An amorphous solid, often transparent substance made by melting sand with a mixture of soda, potash and lime.
definitions.example: A popular myth is that window glass is actually an extremely viscous liquid.

definitions.definition: A vessel from which one drinks, especially one made of glass, plastic, or similar translucent or semi-translucent material.
definitions.example: Fill my glass with milk, please.

definitions.definition: (metonymically) The quantity of liquid contained in such a vessel.
definitions.example: There is half a glass of milk in each pound of chocolate we produce.

definitions.definition: Glassware.
definitions.example: We collected art glass.

definitions.definition: A mirror.
definitions.example: She adjusted her lipstick in the glass.

definitions.definition: A magnifying glass or telescope.
A barrier made of solid, transparent material.
A barometer.
(in names of species) Transparent or translucent.
definitions.example: glass frog;  glass shrimp;  glass worm

definitions.definition: An hourglass.
Lenses, considered collectively.
definitions.example: Her new camera was incompatible with her old one, so she needed to buy new glass.

definitions.definition: To fit with glass; to glaze.
To enclose in glass.
To strike (someone), particularly in the face, with a drinking glass with the intent of causing injury.
To bombard an area with such intensity (nuclear bomb, fusion bomb, etc) as to melt the landscape into glass.
To view through an optical instrument such as binoculars.
To smooth or polish (leather, etc.), by rubbing it with a glass burnisher.
To reflect; to mirror.
To make glassy.
To become glassy.
The plant Solanum melongena.
The edible fruit of the Solanum melongena: an aubergine.
A dark purple color, like that of the skin of this fruit.
A black person (used mainly by Italian-Americans).
A 180 backside rotated invert in which the front hand is planted on the lip of the halfpipe wall
The main tower of a castle or fortress, located within the castle walls.
The food or money required to keep someone alive and healthy; one's support, maintenance.
definitions.example: He works as a cobbler's apprentice for his keep.

definitions.definition: The act or office of keeping; custody; guard; care; heed; charge; notice.
The state of being kept; hence, the resulting condition; case.
definitions.example: to be in good keep

definitions.definition: That which is kept in charge; a charge.
A cap for holding something, such as a journal box, in place.
To continue in (a course or mode of action); not to intermit or fall from; to uphold or maintain.
definitions.example: to keep silence;  to keep one's word;  to keep possession

definitions.definition: (heading) To hold the status of something.
(heading) To hold or be held in a state.
To wait for, keep watch for.
To act as wicket-keeper.
definitions.example: Godfrey Evans kept for England for many years.

definitions.definition: To take care; to be solicitous; to watch.
To be in session; to take place.
definitions.example: School keeps today.

definitions.definition: To observe; to adhere to; to fulfill; not to swerve from or violate.
(by extension) To visit (a place) often; to frequent.
To observe or celebrate (a holiday).
definitions.example: The feast of St. Stephen is kept on December 26.

definitions.definition: A car with automatic transmission.
definitions.example: I never learned to drive a stick. I can only drive an automatic.

definitions.definition: A semi-automatic pistol.
definitions.example: The G-men raiding the speakeasy were equipped with .45 automatics, while the local policemen were carrying revolvers and shotguns.

definitions.definition: Capable of operating without external control or intervention.
definitions.example: The automatic clothes washer was a great labor-saving device.

definitions.definition: Done out of habit or without conscious thought.
definitions.example: Absent-minded doodling is a form of automatic art.

definitions.definition: Necessary, inevitable, prescribed by logic, law, etc.
definitions.example: Spitting at another player means an automatic red card.

definitions.definition: (of a firearm such as a machine gun) Firing continuously as long as the trigger is pressed until ammunition is exhausted.
definitions.example: Fully automatic weapons cannot be legally owned by private citizens in the US, except in very special circumstances, as by private security companies.

definitions.definition: (of a handgun) An autoloader; a semi-automatic or self-loading pistol, as opposed to a revolver or other manually actuated handgun, which fires one shot per pull of the trigger; distinct from machine guns.
definitions.example: The US Army adopted John Browning's M1911 pistol as its sidearm, chambered in .45 ACP (Automatic Colt Pistol).

definitions.definition: (of a local variable) Automatically added to and removed from the stack during the course of function calls.
(of a group) Having one or more finite-state automata
A large flat surface, finished with black slate or a similar material, that can be written upon with chalk and subsequently erased; a chalkboard.
To use a blackboard to assist in an informal discussion.
(sometimes proscribed) To tweet; to post an update to Twitter.
The sound of a succession of chirps as uttered by birds.
definitions.example: I often listen to the twitter of the birds in the park.

definitions.definition: A tremulous broken sound.
A slight trembling of the nerves.
Unwanted flicker that occurs in interlaced displays when the image contains vertical detail that approaches the horizontal resolution of the video format.
To utter a succession of chirps.
(of a person) To talk in an excited or nervous manner.
To make the sound of a half-suppressed laugh; to titter; to giggle.
To have a slight trembling of the nerves; to be excited or agitated.
To twit; to reproach or upbraid.
The details or facts; especially, those obtained by gossip or rumor.
definitions.example: She called to get the skinny on the latest goings-on in the club.

definitions.definition: A state of nakedness; nudity.
A low-fat serving of coffee.
A skinny being.
To reduce or cut down.
Thin, generally in a negative sense (as opposed to slim, which is thin in a positive sense).
definitions.example: Her recent weight loss has made her look rather skinny than slender

definitions.definition: (of food or beverages) Low-fat.
Naked; nude (chiefly used in the phrase skinny dipping).
(of clothing) tight-fitting
(manner) At a slow pace.
A conversation or other form of discourse between two or more individuals.
definitions.example: Bill and Melinda maintained a dialogue via email over the course of their long-distance relationship.

definitions.definition: (authorship) In a dramatic or literary presentation, the verbal parts of the script or text; the verbalizations of the actors or characters.
definitions.example: The movie had great special effects, but the dialogue was lackluster.

definitions.definition: A literary form, where the presentation resembles a conversation.
definitions.example: A literary historian, she specialized in the dialogues of ancient Greek philosophers.

definitions.definition: A dialogue box.
definitions.example: Once the My Computer dialogue opens, select Local Disk (C:), then right click and scroll down.

definitions.definition: To discuss or negotiate so that all parties can reach an understanding.
definitions.example: Pearson wanted to dialogue with his overseas counterparts about the new reporting requirements.

definitions.definition: To put into dialogue form.
To take part in a dialogue; to dialogize.
To enthusiastically affirm or congratulate a person, especially by patting them on the back.
Action of the verb to backslap
The two-note sound made by the cuckoo.
A Barbadian food made from mashed okra and cornmeal.
Any of various birds, of the family Cuculidae, famous for laying its eggs in the nests of other species; but especially the common cuckoo, Cuculus canorus, that has a characteristic two-note call.
The sound of that particular bird.
The bird-shaped figure found in cuckoo clocks.
The cuckoo clock itself.
A person who inveigles themselves into a place where they should not be (used especially in the phrase a cuckoo in the nest).
Someone who is crazy.
To make the call of a cuckoo.
To repeat something incessantly.
Crazy; not sane.
Something that impedes, stands in the way of, or holds up progress
definitions.example: A big obstacle to understanding the manual was that it had been poorly translated from the Japanese.

definitions.definition: A room, especially one which is not particularly large, used for manufacturing or other light industrial work.
A brief, intensive course of education for a small group, emphasizing interaction and practical problem solving.
An academic conference.
To help a playwright revise a draft of (a play) by rehearsing it with actors and critiquing the results.
To work on or revise something, especially collaboratively, in a workshop.
To improve through collaboration.
The bony cavity which contains the eyeball and its associated muscles, vessels, and nerves. In humans, seven bones make up each bony orbit.
A person who studies or learns about a particular subject.
definitions.example: He is a student of life.

definitions.definition: A person who is formally enrolled at a school, a college or university, or another educational institution.
Deserving of merit or commendation; deserving reward.
definitions.example: The policeman received the Award of Meritorious Service from his grateful department.

definitions.definition: A collective ensemble of eating and serving utensils such as knives, forks and spoons.
The business of a cutler.
A person who occupies an office or a position.
definitions.example: I cannot say the same of the current occupant of the position

definitions.definition: A person who occupies a place.
The owner or tenant of a property.
A series of events which produce a result (the product).
definitions.example: This product of last month's quality standards committee is quite good, even though the process was flawed.

definitions.definition: A set of procedures used to produce a product, most commonly in the food and chemical industries.
definitions.example: 1960, Mack Tyner, Process Engineering Calculations: Material and Energy Balances – Ordinarily a process plant will use a steam boiler to supply its process heat requirements and to drive a steam-turbine generator.

definitions.definition: A path of succession of states through which a system passes.
Successive physiological responses to keep or restore health.
Documents issued by a court in the course of a lawsuit or action at law, such as a summons, mandate, or writ.
An outgrowth of tissue or cell.
A structure that arises above a surface.
An executable task or program.
The centre mark that players aim at in the game of squails.
To perform a particular process on a thing.
To retrieve, store, classify, manipulate, transmit etc. (data, signals, etc.), especially using computer techniques.
definitions.example: We have processed the data using our proven techniques, and have come to the following conclusions.

definitions.definition: To think about a piece of information, or a concept, in order to assimilate it, and perhaps accept it in a modified state.
definitions.example: I didn't know she had a criminal record. That will take me a while to process.

definitions.definition: To develop photographic film.
To take legal proceedings against.
Return to the previous state.
definitions.example: The server is unstable, we need to rollback the changes.

definitions.definition: Sensorium
An organ or faculty of sense.
Of the senses or sensation.
The trunk of a dead tree, cleared of branches.
definitions.example: They walked across the stream on a fallen log.

definitions.definition: Any bulky piece as cut from the above, used as timber, fuel etc.
A unit of length equivalent to 16 feet, used for measuring timber, especially the trunk of a tree.
Anything shaped like a log; a cylinder.
A floating device, usually of wood, used in navigation to estimate the speed of a vessel through water.
A blockhead; a very stupid person.
A heavy longboard.
A rolled cake with filling.
A weight or block near the free end of a hoisting rope to prevent it from being drawn through the sheave.
A piece of feces.
A penis.
To cut trees into logs.
To cut down (trees).
To cut down trees in an area, harvesting and transporting the logs as wood.
Actions performed to keep some machine or system functioning or in service.
A tort committed when a third party who does not have a bona fide interest in a lawsuit provides help or acquires an interest to a litigant's lawsuit.
Alimony, a periodical payment or a lump sum made or ordered to be made to a spouse after a divorce.
Child support.
Money required or spent to provide for the needs of a person or a family.
The natural process which keeps an organism alive.
To relate (a story or series of events) in speech or writing.
To give an account.
The most remote and desolate areas of Australia; the desert and areas too arid for growing crops.
Characteristic of the most remote and desolate areas of Australia; very remote from urban areas.
To or towards the most remote and desolate areas of Australia.
A person with traditional beliefs.
Short for traditional Chinese.
Short for traditional art.
Short for traditional grip.
Of, relating to, or derived from tradition.
definitions.example: I think her traditional values are antiquated.

definitions.definition: Communicated from ancestors to descendants by word only.
definitions.example: traditional expositions of the Scriptures.

definitions.definition: Observant of tradition; attached to old customs; old-fashioned.
In lieu of the name of the composer of a piece of music, whose real name is lost in the mists of time.
Relating to traditional Chinese.
definitions.example: The traditional form of the character has twice as many strokes as the simplified form.

definitions.definition: A philosophy that rejects the use of violence, and instead seeks to bring about change through peaceful responses even to violent acts.
Downward movement, fall.
A sloping downward, e.g. of a hill or road.
A weakening.
A reduction or diminution of activity.
The act of declining or refusing something.
To move downwards, to fall, to drop.
definitions.example: The dollar has declined rapidly since 2001.

definitions.definition: To become weaker or worse.
definitions.example: My health declined in winter.

definitions.definition: To bend downward; to bring down; to depress; to cause to bend, or fall.
To cause to decrease or diminish.
To turn or bend aside; to deviate; to stray; to withdraw.
definitions.example: a line that declines from straightness

definitions.definition: To refuse, forbear.
definitions.example: On reflection I think I will decline your generous offer.

definitions.definition: (grammar, usually of substantives, adjectives and pronouns) To inflect for case, number and sometimes gender.
(by extension) To run through from first to last; to repeat like a schoolboy declining a noun.
To reject a penalty against the opposing team, usually because the result of accepting it would benefit the non-penalized team less than the preceding play.
definitions.example: The team chose to decline the fifteen-yard penalty because their receiver had caught the ball for a thirty-yard gain.

definitions.definition: Consisting of flakes or of small, loose masses; lying, or cleaving off, in flakes or layers; flakelike.
(of a person) Unreliable; likely to make plans with others but then abandon those plans.
(of a thing) Unreliable; working only on an intermittent basis; likely to malfunction.
definitions.example: I cannot enjoy the online game because of my flaky Internet connection.

definitions.definition: A very large meal, often of a ceremonial nature.
definitions.example: We had a feast to celebrate the harvest.

definitions.definition: Something delightful
definitions.example: It was a feast for the eyes.

definitions.definition: A festival; a holy day or holiday; a solemn, or more commonly, a joyous, anniversary.
Water that exists beneath the earth's surface in underground streams and aquifers.
Shining, brilliant.
Of language, speeches etc: lucid, brilliantly clear.
A task.
definitions.example: A job half done is hardly done at all.

definitions.definition: An economic role for which a person is paid.
definitions.example: He's been out of a job since being made redundant in January.

definitions.definition: (in noun compounds) Plastic surgery.
definitions.example: He had had a nose job.

definitions.definition: A task, or series of tasks, carried out in batch mode (especially on a mainframe computer).
A sudden thrust or stab; a jab.
A public transaction done for private profit; something performed ostensibly as a part of official duty, but really for private gain; a corrupt official business.
Any affair or event which affects one, whether fortunately or unfortunately.
A thing (often used in a vague way to refer to something whose name one cannot recall).
definitions.example: Pass me that little job with the screw thread on it.

definitions.definition: To do odd jobs or occasional work for hire.
To work as a jobber.
To take the loss.
To buy and sell for profit, as securities; to speculate in.
(often with out) To subcontract a project or delivery in small portions to a number of contractors.
definitions.example: We wanted to sell a turnkey plant, but they jobbed out the contract to small firms.

definitions.definition: To seek private gain under pretence of public service; to turn public matters to private advantage.
To strike or stab with a pointed instrument.
To thrust in, as a pointed instrument.
To hire or let in periods of service.
definitions.example: to job a carriage

definitions.definition: The set of letters used when writing in a language.
definitions.example: In the first year of school, pupils are taught to recite the alphabet.

definitions.definition: A writing system in which letters represent phonemes. (Contrast e.g. logography, a writing system in which each character represents a word, and syllabary, in which each character represents a syllable.)
A typically finite set of distinguishable symbols.
definitions.example: Let L be a regular language over the alphabet \Sigma.

definitions.definition: An individual letter of an alphabet; an alphabetic character.
The simplest rudiments; elements.
To designate by the letters of the alphabet; to arrange alphabetically.
The act of notifying.
A specific piece of information that serves to notify.
A text message on a cell phone.
A type of music and dance that originated in the West Indies (perhaps Trinidad), characterized by improvised lyrics on topical or broadly humorous subjects, often creating satire of current events.
To perform calypso.

A film, or some other dramatic work, set in, the historic (c. 1850-1910) American West (west of the Mississippi river) focusing on conflict between whites and Indians, lawmen and outlaws, ranchers and farmers, or industry (railroads, mining) and agriculture.
Of, facing, situated in, or related to the west.
definitions.example: the western approaches

definitions.definition: (of a wind) Blowing from the west; westerly.
Occidental.
Awe-inspiring; terrific.
Notable for its size, power, or excellence.
definitions.example: Van Beethoven's ninth symphony is a tremendous piece of music.

definitions.definition: Extremely large (in amount, extent, degree, etc.) or great
definitions.example: There was a tremendous outpouring of support.

definitions.definition: One who gardens; one who grows plants or cultivates a garden.
definitions.example: "Ponder the fact that God has made you a gardener, to root out vice and to plant virtue." — St. Catherine of Siena

definitions.definition: Dissatisfied and irritable.
definitions.example: The old man was grumpy because he couldn’t find his glasses.

definitions.definition: Dirt, grease, soot, etc. that is ingrained and difficult to remove.
definitions.example: Underneath all that soot, dirt and grime is the true beauty of the church in soft shades of sandstone.

definitions.definition: A genre of urban music that emerged in London, England, in the early 2000s, primarily a development of UK garage, dancehall, and hip hop.
To begrime; to cake with dirt.
To hire (somebody for work or a job).
definitions.example: Yesterday our local garage employed a new mechanic.

definitions.definition: To use (somebody for a job, or something for a task).
definitions.example: The burglar employed a jemmy to get in.

definitions.definition: To make busy.
Healthy.
definitions.example: He was safe and sound.

definitions.definition: Complete, solid, or secure.
definitions.example: Fred assured me the floorboards were sound.

definitions.definition: Having the property of soundness.
Good; acceptable; decent.
definitions.example: "How are you?" - "I'm sound."

definitions.definition: (of sleep) Quiet and deep. Sound asleep means sleeping peacefully, often deeply.
definitions.example: Her sleep was sound.

definitions.definition: Heavy; laid on with force.
definitions.example: a sound beating

definitions.definition: Founded in law; legal; valid; not defective.
definitions.example: a sound title to land

definitions.definition: Soundly.
Yes; used to show agreement or understanding, generally without much enthusiasm.
